suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `window-controls-worker` guidance so startup-stabilization and bootstrap/titlebar tasks explicitly call out early platform-resolution and `@tauri-apps/plugin-os` crashes as a first diagnostic path, and clarify that process/log verification can satisfy narrowly scoped launch-stabilization work before later desktop validators take over.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `validation-surface-launch-stabilization` and `renderer-role-and-control-binding` both reported justified deviations from manual desktop verification because startup was blocked by early `@tauri-apps/plugin-os` access before Tauri OS internals were ready.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `window-lifecycle-worker` guidance to explicitly allow automated-only completion for backend-only close-accounting work when Rust lifecycle regressions fully cover the behavior and desktop-surface validation is delegated to dedicated validation features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `window-close-accounting` completed with focused Rust regression coverage and a justified deviation from the skill's blanket manual desktop-validation step.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit Windows mission guidance that `.factory/init.sh` should be read and translated to Windows-native commands rather than executed directly when it is a POSIX shell script.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `validation-surface-launch-stabilization` attempted to execute `.factory/init.sh` under PowerShell before falling back to reading it; the worker handoff recorded this as a preventable procedure deviation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document the recurring `@tauri-apps/plugin-os` startup failure in mission shared-state docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: recommend-to-orchestrator instead of apply-now because it affects mission/library guidance outside this validator's additive factual updates and duplicates the broader skill-guidance recommendation.
